title: See all assigned tasks in My Week without dates or deadlines
excerpt:
Hello, 
We have 12 boards for different clients, and team of 7 designers. We have about 10-15 new design tickets every day, across all boards. Most of them doesn’t have date set, and some have future date, like in a 2-3 …

posts.author: KamilMark (Kamil Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

We have 12 boards for different clients, and team of 7 designers. We have about 10-15 new design tickets every day, across all boards. Most of them doesn’t have date set, and some have future date, like in a 2-3 weeks. Because of that, a large number of important tasks isn’t visible in my week.

What I would love to have in monday, is another view similar to My Week, but based on all task what are assigned to me (current user - default view) or to another member of the team, or all members (so we can see load).
Tasks should be sorted on status, with “done” ones at the bottom (like in My Week)

For my team this is critical problem, because it’s very time consuming to check every board, set proper filters, etc. I know I can make multi-board table in Dashboards, but 1) we have limit to 10 connected boards and 2) it’s not very convenient to analyze. There were a few people posting in FB group about something similar, so I think this is important. On the other hand, I think it’s relatively easy to develop since it can be My Week module, but with different data source.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for the suggestion, Kamil. We’ll definitely take this away to think about. I think that for now, using the search everything would be a better way to see everything assigned to you for a specific date period, using the Filter by date option, once you’ve searched for your name.

Appreciate it won’t sort the tasks by their status in the results but it does give you that overview of all tasks assigned within a date period which you’ve been assigned to and the search can be saved for next time.

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bump up for us
The MY WEEK interface needs some improvements.
For the record, we’re in a similar situation than KamilMark. We use the filter in the MY WEEK which helps a bit but I too see a lot of room of improvement
Many of us use Monday.com for task management (among other things). It’s quite essential to have a quick and easy dashboard view that will list my tasks with a filtering, sorting and saving capabilities.
Danny

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Yep, I agree massively on this., right now the android app is doing this better as it shows the status column in My Week, which is really helpful. That would be a good start.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, I wanted to say thanks for making it that the status shows up in My Week on the computer now. Really helpful!

I wonder if you could tweak the layout? As a user, I want to be able to see the full item name when scrolling My Week so I don’t have to click the item to see what it is.

My Week layout.PNG2713×1512 374 KB

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a user, I want to be able to stay in My Week as much as possible instead of switching to other boards, forgetting where I’m at, and then switching back to My Week.

To help with that, I would like to be able to create Items directly from My Week.

  • Have a button with New Item
  • Then you name the item
  • Then you choose which board, and then which group it goes on.
  • Then a popup card would show like when you click an Item in My Week. And on that I could add the other information depending on what columns are on the board.

This would be really helpful for quickly adding new tasks, adding new contacts to our contacts board and more.

posts.author: KamilMark (Kamil Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to have option to change my week functionality to view people instead of dates. So basically without date filters at the top, and instead of Previous Weeks, Earlier this week etc, see John Doe, Jane Doe etc tabs, and their not-done tasks (sorted by status or date).

Also, it would be nice if clicking on status in my week show only dropdown with other statuses to change, instead of popup with all the things.

posts.author: alexmrenn (Alex Renn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Could there be a “board view” in my week? That shows all tasks essentially like the search results, but with only four columns: deadline, status tied to deadline, assignee, and task name?

If you could view this and allow filtering on just those columns, this would become incredibly powerful. Then you could apply the board filtering to this board, to search within the task list, filter by date, assignee, status, etc.

My biggest problems with myweek as it stands: the modules are oversized, so I have to do a lot of scrolling to get everything on screen (the boards are great, why throw that away?), collapsing doesn’t persist, and it takes three clicks to get to the discussion on a task.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
the modules are oversized, so I have to do a lot of scrolling to get everything on screen

Agreed.

If I could change just one thing right now, it would be to introduce a Compact View to get more pulses on screen at once and save scrolling. Bugs me every day.

posts.author: jakub-reborn (Jakub)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news? My week tab is no showing anything that is in progress but only due dates. That creates lack of any summary space like in jira : ] Everything like @KamilMark said – I’m supporting this request 100%

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with the gist of this thread (and many others with My Week requests / concerns). My Week needs some significant improvements in functionality. This should be one of the most powerful, customizable, and easy-to-use tools of the app. Like Asana and other apps, Monday needs a simple and comprehensive one-stop place for users to view and interact with all of the tasks they are assigned to, and with easy configuration options to see assigned tasks the way the user wants to see them. This should have the same customization capabilities as the dashboard table widget (for boards, groups, filters, etc.), but with a predefined structure and focus on the tasks the user is assigned to.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
hdurey:

My Week needs some significant improvements in functionality.

Agreed.

There’s currently lots of dev time going into WFH and remote working initiatives.

Whilst Monday.com is doing a great job, I’d really like to see the core features improved at the same time.

There are 12 months of excellent feature requests here in the forums waiting for some love. Sub-items are clearly the current hot topic behind the scenes but there are also some ‘low cost’ feature improvements waiting in the green room.

posts.author: nikolamujdzic (Nikola Mujdzic Rescic)
posts.cookedHtml: agree and support. Long time no reply from dev team

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got super frustrated today with My Week. The user experience is just very sub-par in so many small ways.

I would love to hear an update on what Monday.com is doing about this. So many of your competitors are nailing their My Week equivalents.

posts.author: Concept (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
JohnW:

ent feature requests here in the forums waiting for some love. Sub-items are clearly the current hot topic behind t

I agree. Myweek is very inefficient!

posts.author: NickVerri (Nick Kennedy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yep, I’m totally with you on this. There is loads to love about Monday.com but My Week is a mess.

Fundementally I don’t trust it to show me everything I have to do this week. It misses stuff I didn’t complete last week

We used to work with wrike.com and while they got a lot of stuff wrong, the My Tasks bit was perfect. You had a bit list of everything assigned to you, sorted into a series of groups, overdue, today later this week and further in the future. You could also create new tasks for yourself within My tasks and post them to any board. (if you didn’t put a date in, it would automatically give it todays date and then you wouldn’t lose it). I’d love to see this all come to Monday. As others have said without this being overhauled its hard to use to generally manage my work without feeling something is gonna get missed/forgotten.

posts.author: Wesley (Wesley Gant)
posts.cookedHtml:
Excellent summary! This is perhaps our biggest pinpoint with Monday. The kind of custom view settings and filter options we get on boards and dashboards is stripped away on MyWeek. Users need a place to start their day, and that place should allow them to see everything on their plate organized however it works best for them—by date, by status, by board, by priority, etc. The timeline, kanban, and workload views would be great here.

Perhaps the original vision for “MyWeek” was much smaller than its potential, hence the name. But with all the customization tools available Monday, the paltry settings panel for this key feature is a huge miss. Count me in for a total overhaul.

posts.author: Tai_Freitas (Tainá)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Julia, do you have any updates about this improvement?
Monday.com Community is full of requests similar to this one and we’re in 2021 already. I believe Monday says this platform is all about visual and practical project and tasks management, so how could you not have released this simple and basic feature yet? Please help us speed this up, considering it’s been more than 1,5 years and the number of costumers requesting it is constantly increasing.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Tai_Freitas you can find an update on this topic here See all my tasks in one place? - #68 by TRB-monday.com.

posts.author: zaneta (Zaneta Grant)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! I’m thrilled to announce that our product team is currently working on revamping My Week with a new solution called My Work. This new feature will show items assigned across all boards, regardless of dates being assigned or not. It is currently in beta testing. You can try it out on your own account by navigating to the menu from your profile picture in the bottom right > monday.labs. Activate the feature called “My work replace my week.”

Feel free to come back and post your feedback about this feature in the thread. Our product team would love to hear what you have to say!

posts.author: Wesley (Wesley Gant)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately, the new MyWork solves almost none of the problems mentioned in this thread. People talk about needing a more compact view (MyWork fits 5 tasks on my browser window), and needing to view different team members’ tasks (you’d have to filter every board), and needing to see tasks in the configuration most helpful to the user (MyWork forces a date-assigned order).

The only thing MyWork solves is not limiting items to this week. But the ordering makes no sense and having to set filters for every board individually in order to see a clean task list… frustratingly tedious to filter and maintain. Honestly, MyWeek is better than this.

What we need is very simple: Starting with the current MyWeek… but expand the range a few weeks, and let me group/sort by status or date or board, instead of forcing “today,” “tomorrow,” etc. (which also takes a lot of space). Maybe there’s a separate section for items that a timeline indicates I should be working on, but aren’t actually “due” for more than 7 days. And Maybe allow people to create their own groups, so they can move items around Kanban-style.

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: Did you remove this option? It is no shown in monday.com labs for me

posts.author: emcarr (Emilie Carr)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m also wondering if My Work was removed as an option. I’d like to try it out, as I’m having similar frustrations with my work being spread across many workspaces/boards and not being easily visible in one spot. @zaneta

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml:
It looks like My Work is being actively developed. Today I logged in and have this showing up. I don’t think I activated My Work from the Labs.

One main problem for me is that sub-items aren’t showing in My Week yet. So it looks like I have nothing due today or this week.

There is a support page here: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360019300579 but this is about the old My Work design . This new design is ordered by date due rather than date assigned - thank goodness.
